title: calystegia spithamaea subsp. stans
intro: Calystegia spithamaea subsp. stans, commonly known as the wild morning glory or woodbine, is a perennial vine in the morning glory family native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Calystegia spithamaea subsp. stans include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to treat a variety of ailments, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
